summary:
SMAD6 variants disrupt BMP signaling and are strongly linked to non-syndromic craniosynostosis, particularly affecting sagittal and metopic sutures. The available literature confirms these variants as significant genetic causes of craniosynostosis.  

\boxed{\text{A}}

\boxed{\text{A}}. Answer choice: A
